auth.heroHeadline: Build a culture that spots risk early—and responds with confidence.

support.title: Support Center
support.subtitle: Get help with your cybersecurity learning journey
support.faq1q: How do I reset my password?
support.faq1a: You can reset your password by clicking the "Forgot Password" link on the login page.
support.faq2a: XP points are awarded based on challenge completion, accuracy, and time taken. Bonus points are given for streaks.
support.faq3a: Yes, you can retake any challenge to improve your score and understanding.
support.faq4q: How do I track my organization's progress?
support.faq4a: Administrators can access detailed analytics and progress reports through the admin dashboard.
support.submitAlert: Support request submitted! We'll get back to you within 24 hours.

challenges.pageSubtitle: Pass score {pct}%+ · XP from completed challenges
challenges.topicLevelsP1: Within each topic (e.g. Phishing, Password), harder levels stay locked until easier ones are done — only among challenges you can see.
challenges.topicLevelsLi1: Intermediate unlocks after every visible Beginner challenge in that topic is passed ({pct}%+ or pass).
challenges.topicLevelsLi2: Advanced unlocks after every visible Intermediate in that topic is passed.
challenges.topicLevelsLi3: If a topic has no beginner (or intermediate) challenges for you, that step is skipped.
challenges.moduleListSubtitle: Explore our full catalog of security training
challenges.popularModulesSubtitle: High-usage, highly rated modules across the platform. Trusted by teams to cover essential topics.
challenges.activeProgressAria: Step {current} of {total}. Question {current} of {total}. {remaining} questions left in this attempt, including the current one.

remediation.title: Review mistakes
remediation.subtitle: Steps you answered incorrectly on past attempts. Retry the challenge to improve your score.
remediation.loadingAttempts: Loading your attempts from the server…
remediation.serverError: Could not load attempts from the server. Showing locally stored attempts only, if any.
remediation.noAttempts: No challenge attempts on your account yet.
remediation.emptyState: No missed steps recorded yet. Complete challenges (with the app connected to the server) to build your review list.

leaderboard.scopeHelpDept: Learners in your department only. Ranked by XP.
leaderboard.needsOrg: Your account is not linked to an organization in Practikal. Ask an admin to assign you to an org to see the live leaderboard.
leaderboard.apiOff: API mode is off — showing only your profile stats. Enable the gamification API to load your organization leaderboard.
leaderboard.emptyScope: No learners in this scope yet — complete a challenge to appear here.
leaderboard.chooseScope: Choose a scope above, or link your account to an organization to load rankings.
leaderboard.unavailable: Leaderboard is unavailable. Try again later.
leaderboard.nextTierWithXp: Next tier: {rank} — {xp} XP remaining in this tier.

achievements.title: Achievements
achievements.subtitleOff: Gamification API is off — static list only. Enable the API for live progress.
achievements.loadError: Could not load achievements. Try again later.
achievements.localNote: Training stats above use your local progress record.
achievements.emptyCatalog: No achievements are in the catalog yet. An admin can add definitions in the database or seed data.
achievements.certFooter: This document was produced from your training record for awareness purposes only.

admin.accessDeniedBody: You don't have permission to access the admin panel.
admin.overviewSubtitle: Scoped training metrics and charts for your organization. Use the sidebar for detailed admin tools and workflows.

roleHierarchy.title: System roles (reference)
roleHierarchy.intro: Typical scope from broad to narrow in this product:
roleHierarchy.defaultAccessNote: Default learner access: approved Staff or Internal accounts receive the baseline learner permission set on every login when no organizational role assignment applies (or the assignment grants no permissions). That covers dashboard, challenges, play, remediation, leaderboard, achievements, and support—no manual permission matrix entry is required for standard learning. Admin types below add broader capabilities on top.
roleHierarchy.step1: Super Admin — full platform control, tenant management, and high-trust approvals.
roleHierarchy.step2: Organization Admin — settings and workflows for the entire organization (tenant).
roleHierarchy.step3: Branch / Unit Admin — a structural branch or unit in the hierarchy (e.g. region, district, outlet).
roleHierarchy.step4: Department Admin — heads one department: roster, team-scoped work, and department tools.
roleHierarchy.step5: Staff — default role for learners and operators; baseline app access is granted as above unless a custom org role restricts or extends it.
roleHierarchy.step6: Self-registration — new users may appear under Pending registrations until an administrator approves access.

admin.noReportsTitle: Training analytics are not enabled for this account.
admin.noReportsBody: You can open this page, but charts and the full training report require the VIEW_REPORTS permission. Ask a superadmin to grant it if you need roster and completion analytics.
admin.timePendingLine: Enable time telemetry to show values
admin.recommendationBody: Focus upcoming assignments on {category} to raise weak-area performance.
admin.recommendationFallback: No recommendation yet. More attempts are needed to identify weak areas.
admin.heatmapFootnote: Cells use the same category aggregate for each period until time-series data is available.
admin.heatmapAggregateFootnote: Each row shows current pass-rate coverage only; no monthly history is implied.
admin.upgradeCardBody: Open the detailed roster report for filters, exports, and per-learner progress.
admin.noDeptData: No department tags on users in this roster slice.
admin.noPeople: No people in this scope yet.
admin.categoryChartTitle: Learners with a pass by category
admin.noCategory: No category activity in this scope.
admin.gamificationOffCategory: Enable gamification API to load category breakdown.
admin.gamificationApiOffBanner: Gamification API is off — completion uses the local challenge catalog and may not match production data.
admin.usersLoadError: Could not load users. Check your permissions and try again.
admin.noPeopleMatchHint: Adjust search or filters, or confirm users exist in this scope.
admin.selectSomeoneHint: Choose a person on the left to see training summary from the server. Category breakdown and time on device appear when you select your own account.
admin.otherUserChartsNote: Category charts and session time reflect this browser's learner data and are shown only when you select your own profile.
admin.rosterUnavailableTitle: Training reports are not available for this account
admin.rosterUnavailableDept: Your profile needs both an organization and a department to load a roster.
admin.rosterUnavailableUnit: Your profile needs both an organization and a unit to load a roster.
admin.rosterUnavailableOrg: Your account is not linked to an organization. Contact a platform administrator.

bulkImport.title: Import users from Excel
bulkImport.columnsHint: Required columns: name, email, phone (10 digits), role (e.g. Learner). Optional: password (default {defaultPwd}).
bulkImport.alertWrongFile: Please choose an Excel or CSV file (.xlsx, .xls, .csv).
bulkImport.alertReadError: Could not read the file. Try exporting as CSV from Excel.
bulkImport.alertNoRows: Upload a file with at least one valid row.

pendingUsers.title: Pending registrations
pendingUsers.orgHintNonDept: Users will be added to your organization.
pendingUsers.deptAdminHint: Department is set to your department. Choose branch and role.
pendingUsers.confirmReject: Remove registration for {first} {last}? They will need to register again.

campaigns.gamificationOffBody: Training assignments are stored in the database when the API is enabled. Set VITE_USE_GAMIFICATION_API (default on) and ensure the backend is reachable.
campaigns.accessLead: Creating and listing organization training assignments requires a Superadmin or Organization admin account. The backend only allows these roles to call the assignment API.
campaigns.accessCampaignNote: Your role includes campaign permission, but assignment management is limited to Superadmin / Org admin.
campaigns.campaignTypeOneTimeBody: Schedule a one-time campaign for a defined set of employees.
campaigns.campaignTypeTriggeredBody: Auto-enroll employees into a campaign when a trigger is matched.
campaigns.triggeredComingSoon: Triggered campaign automation will be enabled after backend trigger rules are available.
campaigns.openChallengesHint: Open Learning challenges so the catalog loads from the API.
campaigns.orgScopeHint: Org-wide assignment applies to all learners in that tenant.
campaigns.assignmentsApply: Assignments will apply to your organization ({label}).
campaigns.listLoadError: Could not load assignments. Check permissions and try again.
campaigns.toastEnterTitle: Enter a title for this assignment.
campaigns.toastTriggeredNotReady: Triggered campaigns are not available yet. Please use One Time for now.
campaigns.toastSelectChallenge: Select a challenge (load challenges from the catalog first).

organizations.headerSubtitle: Create tenants, assign org admins, and manage permissions for each organization.
organizations.loadErrorHint: Check that the API is running and you are signed in as a superadmin.
organizations.emptyStateNoOrgsBody: Create your first organization to onboard customers or internal business units.
organizations.modalNewSubtitle: Creates a tenant other users can be assigned to.
organizations.slugPatternTitle: Lowercase letters, numbers, and hyphens only
organizations.slugHint: Unique identifier used in the API and admin tools.
organizations.modalAdminSubtitle: Creates a user tied to {name}. They can sign in with the phone number below.
organizations.passwordChangeHint: Ask them to change it after first login.
organizations.confirmGrantAll: Grant all permissions to this organization?
organizations.toastAllGranted: All permissions granted to this organization.
organizations.confirmResetPassword: Reset this user’s password to the server default? (Password123 unless DEFAULT_PASSWORD is set in the API .env.)

console.permToggleHelp: Toggle modules this organization can use. Click again to revoke.
console.peopleEmpty: No users yet. Use “Add admin” on the organization card to create an org admin.
console.noDepartments: No departments yet. Create them under Branch hierarchy or your dept workflow.
console.noUnits: No units in the tree for this organization.

departmentMgmt.title: Departments & teams
departmentMgmt.intro: Org admins define the horizontal structure (departments) and heads; department heads manage their roster, export staff, and author challenges for their team. Superadmins can work across all tenants.
departmentMgmt.confirmMakeDeptAdmin: Make this person the department admin (head)? Any other department admin in this department becomes a regular staff user.
departmentMgmt.rosterActionsHint: Organization admins: use “Make department admin” on a staff row to assign the department head.
departmentMgmt.howToHeadTitle: How to assign a department head
departmentMgmt.howToHeadIntro: The department head is the person with “department admin” access for that team. As an organization (or unit) admin:
departmentMgmt.howToHeadStep1: Create a department if you have not already.
departmentMgmt.howToHeadStep2: Add people to that department: Add staff, Assign existing user, or Import staff — they must appear in the department roster.
departmentMgmt.howToHeadStep3: Open the roster with Show roster on the department card.
departmentMgmt.howToHeadStep4: Click Make department admin on their row. If someone else was already department admin for that department, they become regular staff.

permissions.title: Permission Engine
permissions.subtitle: Select a capability to manage its cascading rules.
permissions.manageRules: Manage access rules for this capability.
permissions.noBranches: No physical branches built yet. Go to Structural Hierarchy to build your company.
permissions.noTargetBody: Select a permission from the engine list on the left to map its cascading branch rules.
permissions.denyBranchOnlyNotice: Admins can only restrict (DENY) capabilities to specific branches. Granting broadly is handled by Super Admins.
permissions.orgAdminViewOnlyNotice: You have read-only access here. Explore permissions and how they apply across branches; only Super Administrators and branch admins can change allocations.

users.title: Personnel roster
users.confirmResetPassword: Reset this account password to the platform default? The user should sign in with their phone number and the new temporary password.
users.confirmDeactivate: Deactivate this account? They will not be able to sign in until reactivated.
users.toastRoleRequiredOrgAdmin: Select a role for this user (default or custom roles from Role Builder).
users.roleFieldHint: This is the user’s assigned role from Role Builder. To change what each role includes, use Admin → Role Builder and Permissions.
users.roleFieldHintUnitAdmin: Same as for org admins: pick a role defined for your organization. Use Hierarchy & rules → Role Builder to create or edit roles. If the list is empty, add at least one role there first.
users.unitAdminBranchLockedHint: New users are created under your assigned branch.
users.roleOrgMissingHint: Your account is not linked to an organization, so roles cannot load. Sign out and sign in again, or contact support.
users.roleListEmptyHint: No roles exist for this organization yet. Open Role Builder and create one (e.g. “Staff” or “Branch learner”), then return here.
users.toastOrgRequired: Organization is missing from your session. Sign out and sign in again.
users.toastUnitAdminScope: Your account has no branch assigned. Contact an organization admin.
users.defaultPasswordBlurb: Default sign-in password is Password123. Ask the user to change it after first login.
users.unitAdminDeptHint: No department is required here. After sign-in, use Departments to create departments for this organization and assign people as needed.
users.toastUnitAdminNeedsUnit: Select a branch (unit) for this unit administrator.

terminology.shellSubtitleMain: Define vocabulary for your organization’s branch hierarchy (e.g. Region, Zone, Woreda, Branch).
terminology.accountNotLinked: Your account is not linked to an organization. Contact a platform administrator.
terminology.emptyNoLevels: No hierarchy defined yet. Create Level 1 (e.g. Region or Head Office).

hierarchy.shellSubtitleSelect: Select an organization to view and edit its branch tree. Top-level units branch into sub-locations matching your terminology levels.
hierarchy.accountNotLinked: Your account is not linked to an organization. Contact a platform administrator.
hierarchy.toastSuperReadOnly: Superadmin view is read-only. Organization or unit admins create and edit branch locations.
hierarchy.inviteAddUnitAdminTitle: Open Active Roster to add a branch admin for this location
hierarchy.noTerminologyBodyAfter: first and define your levels (e.g. Region, City, Branch), then return here to add locations.
hierarchy.emptyNetworkBodySuper: No locations yet for this organization. The tenant’s organization admin adds the first top-level location from their admin account.
hierarchy.emptyNetworkBodyAdmin: Add the first top-level location (e.g. region or head office). Sub-locations nest under it.
hierarchy.cannotCreateSuper: Superadmin cannot create tenant branches. Switch to an organization admin (or unit admin) account to add locations.
hierarchy.cannotCreateUnitAdmin: Top-level locations are created by your organization administrator. You can add sub-locations under your assigned branch when it appears in this list.

roles.title: Role Management
roles.subtitle: Design custom structural roles strictly using the permissions allocated to your organization.
roles.rolesPageHint: System baseline rows (Learner, Organization Admin, Branch Admin, Department Admin) document typical bundles. Only superadmin may edit baseline permissions here; baseline role names stay fixed for server sync. Tenant custom roles are per-organization and editable by your org/unit admins.
roles.baselineEditRequiresSuperAdmin: Editing system baseline rows (no organization) requires a Super Admin account. As an org or unit admin you can create and edit custom roles for your tenant only.
roles.emptyRoles: No roles defined. Create your first custom role above.
roles.permLockedTitle: Contact Super Admin to unlock this capability
roles.matrixLockedPreview: Locked by permission matrix — admin preview (PLAY_CHALLENGES, VIEW_REMEDIATION) must stay on tenant roles.
roles.hintPickTenant: Choose an organization above before saving — custom roles are created for that tenant.
roles.hintBaselineLockedName: The role name stays fixed so server sync can find this baseline again.
